32” yellow poplar within 2 feet of the garage.Reason for Removal:OtherComments:The tree is creating site conflict conditions due to its size and proximity to the concrete wall.

none Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Undesirable species. The tree is located in the portion of the
alleyway under the purview of the subject property.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Boxelder Tree Size (DBH):17Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:none Reason for Removal:OtherComments:Old rip-out or tear-out at a codominant union. Canopy structure
suggests prior topping. Healed, incised (sunken) cavity with minor
incipient decay at the bottom. Minor decay pockets at older pruning
wounds. Deformed structural root plate, growing against and over
the drop inlet. The tree is located in the portion of the alleyway
under the purview of the subject property.

Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments: The tree has a codominant structure at the base with
approximately 8 ft of included bark. Multiple other codominant
unions are consistent throughout the canopy. The tree is growing
against a fence. On the north side at the fence, the roots appear to
have been severed at some point in the past. Sounding of that
side of the tree was hollow and shell failure occurred during
inspection, exposing advanced decay at the root flare and lower
trunk. A small opening between the root flares on the west side led
to a larger internal cavity. Endoscopic inspection indicates internal
decay. Video uploaded to Accella showing sounding, probing, and
endoscopic inspection.
		
		
			Tree number:2Species:Pecan Tree Size (DBH):29Tree location:see site map Description of Tree:Numbered flagging tape Reason for Removal:OtherComments: In the upper crown, one of the codominant leaders shows a
conglomerate of irregular woundwood forming a pronounced C
shaped wound margin, with woundwood wrapping broadly around
the union. From the property to the south looking north, the last
photo shows this union is sharply codominant with included bark
and suggests this portion may have broken off or been topped at
one time.
